expectations.text: ProductCard 구현이 하나의 source of truth로 정리된다
expectations.evidence: Output contains a single ProductCard.tsx file. Transcript confirms the duplicate components/ProductCard/index.tsx directory was deleted, leaving one canonical implementation.

The executor chose the flat-file pattern as the canonical location. The final output IS components/ProductCard.tsx — the flat file remains as the sole implementation. The assertion expected it to be removed.

The executor placed the consolidated component at components/ProductCard.tsx (flat file), not ProductCard/index.tsx. Transcript explicitly states: 'Keep the flat-file location (components/ProductCard.tsx)'.
Output line 5: 'export interface ProductCardProps' — the interface name is preserved exactly.
Only one ProductCard file exists after consolidation, so all imports would resolve to the same path. Transcript notes no existing import sites were found in app/.

expectations.text: hover, image, price 등 visual affordance가 보존된다
expectations.evidence: Output preserves: hover scale (group-hover:scale-105), shadow transition (shadow-sm hover:shadow-md), aspect-square image container, badge overlay, ko-KR price locale, h3 heading with line-clamp-2, rounded-2xl border radius.

expectations.text: useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, useMutation 호출이 없다
expectations.evidence: The output file contains only a presentational component with no side-effect hooks or data-fetching calls. The only import is 'next/image'.
No folder was created. The executor used a flat file (components/ProductCard.tsx) instead of a folder structure, so there is no folder name to evaluate.
The file is at components/ProductCard.tsx, not under components/common/productCard/. The executor chose to follow the existing flat-file convention rather than the expected common/ placement.

claims.claim: The flat-file pattern is the dominant convention in the project
claims.evidence: Transcript states: 'most components follow the flat .tsx pattern at components/ComponentName.tsx (Button.tsx, ReviewCard.tsx, EmptyState.tsx, etc.)'

claims.claim: The richer ProductCard/index.tsx implementation was used as the canonical source
claims.evidence: Output contains all the features from the folder-based version: imageAlt, badge, testId props, aspect-square, group-hover:scale-105, shadow-sm, ko-KR locale, h3 heading.
Only one output file (ProductCard.tsx) exists, and the transcript confirms components/ProductCard/ directory was deleted.
Transcript mentions running tsc --noEmit but no compiler output is included in the outputs directory to verify independently.

suggestions.reason: This assertion is trivially satisfied when no import sites exist. It passes regardless of whether the component is placed correctly. Consider seeding the test project with actual import statements so the assertion verifies real path consistency.

Four assertions (no-flat-file, folder-index-tsx, folder-camelCase, common-placement) all test aspects of the same structural requirement — place the component at components/common/productCard/index.tsx. These could be collapsed into one or two assertions since they are redundant and fail together.

eval_feedback.overall: The structural assertions form a clear specification that discriminates well — the without-skill run chose a different (flat-file) convention and failed exactly the assertions it should have. The import-path assertion is weak due to absence of real import sites.
